steps.title: Locate the consumption capacity for each whale
steps.content: The passage states the northern right whale “can consume as much as 24,000 kg of krill per day.” For the pygmy right whale, the passage says baleen whales can eat “up to 30 percent of their total mass per day,” and the pygmy right whale's maximum mass is 3,500 kg—so its maximum is 1,050 kg daily.

steps.title: Apply the numbers to the choices
steps.content:
Choice C asserts that the northern right whale eats more krill per day—a claim directly supported by the passage's consumption figures (24,000 kg vs. 1,050 kg).

The other choices either infer unsupported ecological impacts, contradict the consumption figures, or misstate which whales eat krill.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
